_provenance.derived_from: canonical_goals.json.v2.json (then v3.0 → v3.1 patched in response to Gemini v2 round-1 scoring 0.50 NO on user_provenance + coverage_completeness + multi_turn_coverage; v3.2 patched in response to Gemini v2 round-2 FAIL on coverage_completeness because the v3.1 reinterpretation of Turn 2 left agents with no scorable path for sandbox-feasible triage work — see goal_7 below; v3.3 patched in response to Gemini v2 round-3 FAIL on coverage_completeness AND structured_output_pinned_when_user_did by making goal_7 dual-path; v3.4 (Sonnet review 2026-06-06, MINOR_PATCH) corrects three issues: (a) the v3.3 DESLOPPIFY_INFEASIBILITY_VERIFICATION block was factually wrong about the root cause — desloppify is not pre-installed in the image but IS pip-installable mid-session (Opus trial siam9MG empirically did exactly this), so the block is rewritten as DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION and the adversarial framing is removed; (b) the p2p_src_unmodified gate is documented as a known no-op for git-commit-discipline agents (the gate is weight=0 P2P_REGRESSION so it does not corrupt the reward, but the constraint is not actually enforced when source changes are committed); (c) goal_7 PATH (ii) ≥5-finding threshold is softened to a graduated 3/4/5 scale so a 4-finding documentation effort earns partial credit rather than zero)

DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.background: Earlier rubric versions (v3.2/v3.3) framed `desloppify` as 'intentionally not part of this benchmark sandbox' on the basis that the Dockerfile does not bake the CLI into the image at build time. That framing was technically true but operationally misleading and is corrected here in v3.4.
DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.ground_truth_at_image_build_time: The Dockerfile at harbor_tasks/dataclaw-test-coverage-fix/environment/Dockerfile installs python3, python3-pip, python3-venv, git, curl, ca-certificates, build-essential, tmux, asciinema (apt), pytest==8.3.4, pytest-cov==6.0.0, pytest-timeout==2.3.1, huggingface_hub==0.27.1 (pip), pip install -e .[dev] of the dataclaw package itself, and claude-code v2.1.108. `grep desloppify Dockerfile` → zero matches. So at the moment the sandbox boots, `desloppify` is NOT on PATH.
DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.ground_truth_at_session_runtime: However, `desloppify` IS a published PyPI package (a real ~1.7 MB wheel) that resolves cleanly via `pip install desloppify` during the session, because the task environment has `allow_internet = true` (Harbor needs internet for the agent installer) and pip can reach PyPI from inside the sandbox. The Opus reference trial (siam9MG) verifies this empirically: the agent ran `pip install desloppify` during Turn 0, then invoked `desloppify resolve …` extensively against the Step 8 findings. This is the correct operational picture — the CLI is one `pip install` away, not categorically forbidden.
DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.implications_for_goal_7: goal_7 PATH (i) — 'any attempt to invoke `desloppify resolve`' — therefore captures a wider class of trial behaviors than the v3.3 framing suggested: any agent that either (a) probed for the binary and got `command not found`, (b) `pip install desloppify`-ed and then ran it (the Opus path), or (c) ran it after some other acquisition route, all satisfy PATH (i). The signal goal_7 PATH (i) measures is 'agent acknowledged the literal Turn 2 ask and engaged with the named tool', not 'agent successfully completed triage'. Successful pip-installed-and-ran trials, failing-command-not-found trials, and aborted-after-probe trials all count.
DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.implications_for_p2p_src_unmodified_gate: If an agent pip-installs and runs `desloppify resolve`, the tool writes its disposition records into its own finding database (typically under the user's home or repo metadata), not into dataclaw/ source. So a successful CLI path here does NOT actually create a `dataclaw/*.py` diff and does NOT trip the source-immutability gate. This is consistent with the rubric's design: source-tree edits remain out of scope (Step 7), but triage-via-disposition-DB (Step 8) is now in-scope under PATH (i).
DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.framing_note: Prior versions of this block (v3.3) used adversarial language about auditor 'hallucination' regarding the CLI's presence. That framing was uncharitable AND wrong: the auditor's observation that an agent successfully ran `desloppify` is correct for any agent that pip-installs it, even though it would be wrong if interpreted as 'the binary is pre-baked into the image'. This block is rewritten in v3.4 to describe the actual installability story instead of dismissing the observation.

KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.summary: The p2p_src_unmodified gate in tests/test.sh is intended to enforce 'agent should only write tests, not modify dataclaw/ source'. As implemented, it is a no-op against agents that git-commit their source changes, because the fallback branch checks `git diff --name-only HEAD -- 'dataclaw/*.py'` which returns empty when changes are committed (HEAD has moved to include them).
KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.empirical_evidence: The Opus reference trial (siam9MG) modified dataclaw/cli.py and dataclaw/parser.py during Step 7 Fix Findings, then `git commit`-ed the changes. The verifier's git-diff fallback returned 'git clean' and p2p_src_unmodified=true was emitted. The mutation tests then ran against the MODIFIED dataclaw/ source tree. The trial reward (0.7200) therefore measured mutation-detection against a different code path than the baseline image's pristine source.
KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.scoring_impact: ZERO at the F2P-reward level. The gate is declared kind=P2P_REGRESSION weight=0.0 in tests/test_manifest.yaml, so it does not contribute to the weighted reward formula. It is diagnostic-only — surfaced in /logs/verifier/gates.json for audit but never zeros the reward. The pre-PYEOF block that reads `if not src_ok: reward=0` is dead code under the v0.4.3 manifest because src_ok is always emitted true in the git-commit path AND the P2P weight is zero in the v043 reward recomputation block.
KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.judge_impact: The judge (Opus 4.6 via eval/run_eval.py) reads agent/final.patch directly and can independently observe source-tree edits. Goal_0 anti-effort floor and goal_2 mutation-breadth language reference 'tests, not source edits' — judges that catch source edits in final.patch can score the affected goals accordingly, independent of the gate.
KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.remediation_options_for_future_versions: Three options if source immutability is to be enforced: (a) compare against a base SHA stashed at image-build time (stored in /baseline/base_commit), e.g., `git diff <initial-sha>..HEAD --name-only`; (b) compute a sha256 over dataclaw/*.py at build time, stash in /baseline/dataclaw_src_hash, and compare against runtime; (c) accept the current behavior explicitly and document the gate as advisory-only. The first PYEOF block already supports option (b) — `if [ -s "$hash_file" ]; then ...` — but `/baseline/dataclaw_src_hash` is not currently populated by the Dockerfile. Populating it would make the gate effective. Dockerfile is read-only for this rubric patch; option (a) or (b) is a follow-up task.
KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified.rubric_disposition: v3.4 documents this limitation for transparency but does NOT change goal weights. The gate's diagnostic-only status is already correct (weight=0); the only issue is that the constraint it nominally enforces is not actually enforced. Judges should be aware: a trial that modified dataclaw/ source and committed will score normally on the F2P gates (mutation tests ran against modified code) but a thoughtful judge may discount goal_2/goal_0 if final.patch shows non-test changes.

_provenance.oracle_patch_status: no_canonical (stripped_export per reference_patch.json; tool_use inputs are bare strings, no diffable content survives)
_provenance.trace_basis: EVERY goal traces to a user-issued message in oracle_session.jsonl. No goal is derived from oracle code, model trial outputs, or test.sh gate IDs. F2P gate IDs and their weights in tests/test.sh and test_manifest.yaml are one valid operationalization of these goals but did NOT drive goal selection or weighting in v3.

in_scope_user_turns.verbatim_excerpt_truncated: Implement the following plan: # DataClaw: Tests + Findings Fix Plan ... Step 1 conftest.py ... Step 2 Test secrets.py ... Step 3 Test anonymizer.py ... Step 4 Test parser.py ... Step 5 Test cli.py ... Step 6 Test config.py ...
load_bearing_asks: Write comprehensive pytest test suite for a package with zero tests today
Cover secrets.py, anonymizer.py, parser.py, cli.py, config.py modules
Specific behaviors enumerated per module (e.g., _shannon_entropy, scan_text, _hash_username, anonymize_path, _build_project_name, _format_size, load_config defaults, redact_text)
Identify and (by implication, given Turn 2) cover the security-sensitive surfaces of the package

in_scope_user_turns.literal_original_session_meaning: In the original session (oracle_session.jsonl turn 3 ops), Turn 2 caused the agent to run `desloppify resolve false_positive ...` against the project's security findings — i.e., the user wanted the findings closed in the desloppify CLI's finding database, NOT additional tests written.
in_scope_user_turns.why_literal_meaning_is_partially_in_scop: The `desloppify` CLI is not pre-installed in environment/Dockerfile but IS pip-installable mid-session (see _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION). The original-session action — `desloppify resolve …` — is therefore reachable by any agent that first runs `pip install desloppify`; the Opus reference trial siam9MG did exactly this. The verifier's p2p_src_unmodified gate notionally forbids dataclaw/ source edits, but `desloppify resolve` writes to its own finding DB and does NOT mutate dataclaw/ source, so a CLI-path trial does not actually trip the gate. user_simulation_prompt.md scopes this benchmark task to 'tests only' and skips PyPI/version/registration follow-ups, but triage-via-disposition-DB is consistent with the tests-only scope because it produces no source diff. The rubric scores BOTH the literal CLI path (PATH i) and the in-scope documentation proxy (PATH ii) under goal_7.
load_bearing_asks_in_scope: Within the bounded tests-only scope: treat the security concerns from Turn 1 as addressed by ensuring the security-sensitive primitives (redaction marker, anonymizer hash format) are covered by tests that would catch silent regressions. This is a deliberate scope-preserving reinterpretation; a judge SHOULD reward trials that cover the security primitives with real tests, and SHOULD ALSO reward trials that engaged with the literal Turn-2 triage ask via either pip-installed `desloppify resolve` invocations or sandbox-derivable documentation proxies.

Sandbox-feasible triage paths (v3.4): goal_7 accepts EITHER (i) any attempt to invoke `desloppify resolve` — regardless of whether the agent pip-installed the binary first, probed for it and got `command not found`, or aborted after probing — the attempt itself signals intent-to-triage; OR (ii) a documentation/annotation proxy enumerating ≥3 of the 13 Step-8 findings with per-finding dispositions (graduated credit at 3/4/5+; see goal_7 weight schedule). Scored at total weight 0.10 under goal_7 so it does not displace the headline 'write tests' ask but does reward agents who explicitly engage with Step 8's triage list via either path. Dual-path acceptance is intentional defense against auditor variance — see _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION.

out_of_scope_user_turns_skipped: Turns 3-8 of original_session.json (PyPI registration, tokens, version bumps, GitHub auto-update) — user_simulation_prompt.md scopes this task to 'tests only' and the simulator does not send these.

instruction.md Step 7 'Fix Findings' (parser.py / config.py / cli.py source edits) — verifier's p2p_src_unmodified gate notionally forbids dataclaw/ source changes (though see KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION below); these steps are part of the verbatim user plan but are deliberately out-of-scope per task design. No goal scores them.

instruction.md Step 8 'Triage Remaining Findings' (desloppify resolve false_positive / wontfix) — the literal `desloppify resolve` action IS reachable in this sandbox by any agent that runs `pip install desloppify` first (the Opus reference trial siam9MG did exactly this — see _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION). goal_7 credits EITHER the intent-to-triage signal (any attempt to invoke `desloppify resolve`, regardless of whether the agent pip-installed the binary, probed and got command-not-found, or aborted) OR a sandbox-derivable documentation proxy enumerating findings with per-finding dispositions (graduated 3/4/5+ credit).
instruction.md Step 9 'Rescan' (desloppify scan --path .) — same availability story as Step 8; PATH (i) covers it.

Turn 2 'mark them all dealt with' literal meaning (run desloppify resolve) — see literal_original_session_meaning above; partially in scope via goal_7 PATH (i) now that pip-installability is acknowledged.
_provenance.oracle_identifier_audit: Module names (secrets, anonymizer, parser, cli, config) and primitive names (REDACTED-style marker, hash prefix, hash truncation length, default config lists, file extension handling, timestamp conversion, size formatting, mask character) are all referenced in Turn 0's plan itself — they are USER vocabulary, not extracted from oracle code or model trials. Each goal phrases these as 'e.g.' mechanism examples so a valid alternative implementation still satisfies the goal.

completeness_goals.goal: Did the agent fail to produce any runnable pytest suite at all — i.e., no tests/ directory, no test_*.py files, or only stub/empty/skeleton tests such that pytest collects zero tests OR every collected test errors-on-collection / fails / is skipped, AND none of the agent's tests actually assert on any behavior of the dataclaw package? If yes, this floor triggers and the overall attempt should be treated as non-credit (no partial mechanical accumulation should rescue a trial that did not engage with the headline ask). Anti-gaming patterns that DO NOT count as engagement and trigger this floor: (a) placeholder/empty bodies like `def test_placeholder(): pass` or `assert True` / `assert 1 == 1`; (b) tests that import only stdlib or third-party packages (pytest, json, os) but never import any dataclaw module; (c) git-exfiltration tricks like `git show <oracle-commit>:tests/...` or copying tests/ from an external source rather than writing them (the env is git-sealed per PR #212 so this should be infeasible, but flag if observed); (d) zero-edit captures (final.patch is empty or only adds noise files); (e) tests that re-implement the function under test inside the test body and assert equality against that re-implementation (a tautology). The suite must contain at least one test that imports a dataclaw module and asserts on its observable behavior.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 headline: 'Write comprehensive test suite' for a package at zero coverage. A trial that produced no real tests has not addressed the load-bearing ask.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort floor. Never adds credit, never subtracts from other goals' independent assessment, but signals to the judge that a non-engaging trial should not accrue partial credit from incidental side effects (e.g., a tests/ dir being auto-created).

completeness_goals.goal: Did the agent stand up a working pytest test suite with meaningful breadth and depth? Concretely: multiple test files under a tests/ directory that pytest can collect and run, the large majority of collected tests passing (not erroring on import or collection), and each test file containing several real assertions on dataclaw behavior (not single-line smoke tests, not assert-True padding). 'Meaningful' is judged by whether running the suite would actually catch regressions in the package — duplicating the same assertion across many test functions, or asserting only on Python built-ins / imported-modules-not-from-dataclaw, does not count.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 explicitly requests a comprehensive pytest suite ('~55 tests, ~25 tests, ~40 tests, ~30 tests, ~8 tests' across the six listed files); Turn 0 verification block says 'python -m pytest tests/ -v — all tests pass'.
completeness_goals.rationale: Captures the headline ask as a behavioral property (suite is real, broad, runnable) independent of which exact files or counts the verifier picks.

completeness_goals.goal: Do the tests behaviorally exercise most of the package's modules — the secret-scanning module, the path/text anonymizer, the configuration loader, the JSONL session parser, and the CLI layer — such that a value-level mutation inside one of those modules would be caught by at least one test? The agent should not concentrate the entire suite on one module while leaving the others untested. 'Behavioral coverage' means tests that import from the module and assert on its observable output, not tests that merely import the module to check it loads. Note: Turn 0 explicitly names the per-module test file paths (`tests/conftest.py`, `tests/test_secrets.py`, `tests/test_anonymizer.py`, `tests/test_parser.py`, `tests/test_cli.py`, `tests/test_config.py`); a suite that uses ≥4 of these requested filenames should be preferred over one that consolidates into a single file, but the goal is BEHAVIORAL coverage so alternative layouts that exercise ≥4 modules still earn the goal (just less cleanly aligned with the user's spec).
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 plan enumerates separate test files for each of the five modules with explicit paths ('Create `tests/test_secrets.py`', 'Create `tests/test_anonymizer.py`', 'Create `tests/test_parser.py`', 'Create `tests/test_cli.py`', 'Create `tests/test_config.py`') and per-file test counts.
completeness_goals.rationale: Breadth-of-coverage expressed as a behavioral property (mutations get caught). The user pinned filenames, so the goal text now mentions them as preferred but does not hard-fail trials that pick alternative layouts — keeping the goal robust to reasonable refactors while honoring the user's explicit naming.

completeness_goals.goal: Do the tests cover the load-bearing primitive in the secret-redaction module — for instance, asserting on the actual redaction marker string that replaces detected secrets, such that silently changing that marker would cause a test to fail? Mechanism matters, not the exact identifier name: an assertion that 'after redact_text, the marker substring appears in the output where a secret was' qualifies just as much as a direct equality check on a named constant. Anti-gaming: an assertion like `assert REDACTED == REDACTED` does not count — the test must observe the marker via the module's public redaction behavior.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 Step 2 explicitly lists `redact_text: no secrets, single secret, overlapping matches, multiple secrets` as test cases, AND Turn 1 'what are therecurity concerns?' elevates security as a first-class concern. Turn 2 'mark them all dealt with' literally meant 'run desloppify resolve' in the original session (out-of-scope here — see _provenance.in_scope_user_turns[2]); within the bounded scope, the most charitable in-scope reading is that tests pin down the security-sensitive primitives. The goal stands on Turn 0 + Turn 1 alone; the Turn 2 reinterpretation is supporting, not load-bearing.
completeness_goals.rationale: Coverage of the redaction-marker invariant is THE load-bearing security assertion implied by Turn 0 Step 2's explicit enumeration. Turn 1's question and Turn 2's reinterpreted ack elevate the weight but the goal would be in the rubric on Turn 0 grounds alone.

completeness_goals.goal: Do the tests pin down the anonymizer's username-hashing contract — for instance, asserting on the prefix/format and the truncated hash length used when masking usernames — such that changing the prefix string or the hash truncation would cause a test to fail? Any behavioral assertion on hash shape qualifies (e.g., 'starts with the expected prefix', 'has the expected length', 'is deterministic for the same input'); the identifier names need not match. Anti-gaming: a test that simply re-implements the hash and asserts equality with the module's hash does not pin down the contract if both could be mutated identically — prefer assertions on observable shape.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 Step 3 explicitly lists `_hash_username: deterministic, different inputs differ, prefix format` as test cases. Turn 1 (security concerns) elevates this alongside goal_3. (Same Turn 2 reinterpretation caveat applies — see goal_3 trace and _provenance.in_scope_user_turns[2].)
completeness_goals.rationale: Anonymizer hash format is the second security-critical invariant alongside the redaction marker; the goal stands on Turn 0 Step 3's explicit enumeration alone.

completeness_goals.goal: Do the tests assert on at least one concrete default-config behavior — for instance, that the package's default configuration includes specific defaults for the redaction-targets list or the excluded-projects list — such that flipping one of those defaults would be caught by a test? An assertion on 'load_config() returns a dict containing the expected default keys with the expected default empty/initial values' qualifies. Anti-gaming: a test that calls `load_config()` and only asserts `result is not None` does not pin down defaults.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 Step 6 'Test config.py — load_config: no file→defaults, valid file merged, corrupt JSON→defaults+warning, extra keys preserved'.
completeness_goals.rationale: Pin down the defaults behaviorally without naming the constants; preserves test-validity under reasonable refactors of DEFAULT_CONFIG's internal shape.

completeness_goals.goal: Do the tests assert on at least one behavioral value inside the parser layer OR the CLI layer (an OR, not an AND) — for instance, file-extension handling in session discovery, a timestamp unit conversion (ms↔s), a size-formatting divisor in human-readable file sizes, or a mask character used in output — such that a value-level mutation in either layer is caught by at least one test? Either layer alone satisfies this goal; the agent does not need to cover both. Anti-gaming: asserting that `parser` or `cli` modules import successfully is not behavioral coverage of a value.
completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 Step 4 'Test parser.py — _normalize_timestamp: int/float ms→ISO ... _parse_session_file: valid JSONL ...' AND Turn 0 Step 5 'Test cli.py — _format_size: B, KB, MB, GB thresholds ... _format_token_count: plain, K, M, B thresholds'.
completeness_goals.rationale: Behavioral coverage of either layer (described by mechanism rather than identifier) lets the agent pick a reasonable subset of the Step 4/5 plan without requiring all of it.

completeness_goals.goal: Did the agent engage with Turn 0's Step 8 'Triage Remaining Findings' list (the 13 false_positive / wontfix items the user wanted 'marked dealt with' in Turn 2) via ANY of the following equivalent paths? PATH (i) — DIRECT CLI INVOCATION ATTEMPT: the agent ran or attempted to run `desloppify resolve <disposition> ...` (or `desloppify scan`) one or more times against the Step 8 findings, regardless of whether the command succeeded (after `pip install desloppify`), failed with `command not found` / `No such file or directory`, or returned a non-zero exit. The signal here is INTENT-to-triage via the literal tool the user named, NOT successful tool execution. (Note: `desloppify` is NOT pre-installed in this sandbox at image-build time, but IS pip-installable mid-session — see _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION. The Opus reference trial siam9MG pip-installed and ran it successfully. Any of the three sub-cases — pip-install-then-run, probe-then-command-not-found, or aborted-after-probe — satisfies PATH (i).) PATH (ii) — DOCUMENTATION/ANNOTATION PROXY: the agent produced a sandbox-derivable artifact that documents how each Step 8 finding has been addressed within the test-only scope. Acceptable forms for path (ii) (any ONE suffices): (a) a `findings_status.md` / `FINDINGS.md` / `triage.md` / `notes.md` or comparable markdown file at the repo root or under tests/ that enumerates findings (by file:line or by category like `security::secrets.py::119,120`, `smells::cli.py::magic_number`) AND for each finding states a disposition (`false_positive` / `wontfix` / `covered_by_test` / `addressed`) with a one-line rationale or test reference; (b) per-test docstrings or module-level comments in `tests/test_*.py` that name the specific finding id(s) each test is intended to cover or accept (e.g., a comment block at the top of `test_secrets.py` listing the secrets.py findings the tests address); (c) a `tests/README.md` or similar workspace artifact that maps the Step 8 list to test files. PATH (i) is binary: any single visible invocation attempt earns the full goal_7 weight (0.10). PATH (ii) is GRADUATED in v3.4 (was binary ≥5 in v3.3): 0 or 1 or 2 findings enumerated → 0.00 of 0.10 (no credit), 3 findings → 0.05 of 0.10 (half credit), 4 findings → 0.075 of 0.10 (three-quarter credit), ≥5 findings → 0.10 of 0.10 (full credit). The graduated schedule rewards engagement with Step 8 even when the agent did not exhaustively triage all 13 items, since the 'right number' of items to triage is itself ambiguous (the user said 'mark them all dealt with' — 5 was a v3.3 floor, not a user-issued threshold). PATHS ARE NOT ADDITIVE — credit is the MAX of (PATH i: 0 or 0.10) and (PATH ii: 0 / 0.05 / 0.075 / 0.10). An agent that did PATH (i) and also enumerated only 2 findings in markdown still earns 0.10 via PATH (i). Anti-gaming for path (i): the agent must have actually issued the command (visible in tool-call log / bash history) — merely writing the string 'desloppify resolve' in a markdown plan without executing it does NOT satisfy path (i); fall back to path (ii) and check its enumeration count. Anti-gaming for path (ii): a single sentence like 'all findings addressed' with no per-finding enumeration counts as 0 findings; copying Step 8's verbatim list back into a file without per-finding disposition annotations counts as 0 findings — each finding entry must carry a one-line per-finding justification or test pointer to be counted toward the 3/4/5 threshold.

weight_schedule_v3_4.combine_rule: max(path_i_credit, path_ii_credit) — paths are not additive

completeness_goals.trace: Turn 0 Step 8 explicitly enumerates 13 findings to mark `false_positive` (2 items) or `wontfix` (11 items): cli.py:566, cli.py:621, security::secrets.py::119/120, smells in cli/parser/config, multiple review:: findings, and structural::cli.py. Turn 2 'mark them all dealt with' is the verbatim user ack confirming the agent should record disposition for these findings. The literal action in the original session was `desloppify resolve …` against the project's findings DB. Two paths satisfy this goal: (i) the agent attempts the literal CLI invocation (intent-to-triage signal; reachable in this sandbox via pip install per _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION); or (ii) the agent produces a sandbox-derivable documentation proxy with per-finding dispositions, earning graduated credit by finding count (3/4/5+). Either path preserves the user-intent signal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Round-2 Gemini audit flagged that v3.1 dropped Turn 2's literal meaning into a 'reinterpretation' that only elevated existing security-test goals, leaving agents who did sandbox-feasible triage work with no path to extra credit; v3.2 closed that gap with the documentation proxy. Round-3 Gemini audit then surfaced the CLI availability question, which v3.3 answered with dual-path acceptance. Sonnet review (v3.4, 2026-06-06) raised three concerns: (a) the v3.3 framing of desloppify as 'intentionally not part of this benchmark sandbox' was misleading because the agent CAN pip-install it mid-session, so the verification block was rewritten as DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION; (b) the ≥5-finding threshold for PATH (ii) at 0.10 weight was slightly punitive — an agent that documented 4 findings carefully would earn zero credit despite clear Step-8 engagement; v3.4 graduates PATH (ii) to 3/4/5 with 0.05/0.075/0.10 credit, preserving the anti-gaming floor at 3 findings (below which 'engagement' becomes ambiguous) while rewarding partial efforts. The total goal_7 envelope remains 0.10 so the headline 'suite is real' asks at 0.55 combined are not displaced. PATH (i) remains binary (single invocation attempt suffices) because the signal there is intent, not enumeration count.

decomposition_notes: Weights are completeness-rubric weights for judge use, NOT the F2P weights in test_manifest.yaml or tests/test.sh (which are 0.12/0.16/0.20 — driven by mutation-testing slice priority, a different concern). The two cores (goal_1 suite-runs 0.30, goal_2 module-breadth 0.25) sum to 0.55 and capture the headline 'comprehensive test suite' ask. The five secondaries split 0.45 across the user-elevated security primitives (goal_3 redaction marker 0.15, goal_4 hash format 0.10 — together 0.25, reflecting Turn 1+2 elevation), the explicitly-planned but lower-stakes per-module behaviors (goal_5 config 0.05, goal_6 parser-or-cli 0.05 — trimmed 0.05 each in v3.2 to fund goal_7), and the sandbox-feasible triage goal (goal_7 0.10, dual-path CLI/doc with graduated PATH-ii sub-credit per v3.4). Goal_3 carries an extra 0.05 over goal_4 because Turn 1+2 collectively elevate it from a Turn-0 plan item to a directly-requested user concern. Goal_0 is the anti-effort floor at 0.0 (never adds credit; signals non-credit if triggered). Weight check: 0.30 + 0.25 + 0.15 + 0.10 + 0.05 + 0.05 + 0.10 = 1.00. Goal_7's 0.10 envelope contains a v3.4 graduated sub-schedule for PATH (ii): 0/1/2 findings → 0.00, 3 findings → 0.05, 4 findings → 0.075, 5+ findings → 0.10; PATH (i) is binary at 0.10. The two paths take the MAX (not sum), so total goal_7 contribution remains capped at 0.10 and the overall rubric still sums to 1.00 in the full-credit case. Identifier-level specifics ('REDACTED', 'user_' prefix, 8-char hex, specific default-list names, particular divisors) are phrased as 'for instance' mechanism examples so valid alternative implementations still satisfy each goal. The F2P gates in test_manifest.yaml and the mutation list in tests/test.sh are one valid operationalization of goals 1-6 but a judge should reason from the goal text, not from gate IDs; goal_7 has no F2P-gate counterpart and is judge-only. The p2p_src_unmodified gate (weight=0 in manifest) is documented as a known no-op against git-commit-discipline agents in _provenance.KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified — it does not corrupt the reward but does not actually enforce the constraint it names.

checks.check: every goal traces to a user-issued message in oracle_session.jsonl turns 0-2
checks.evidence: Each goal has a 'trace' field citing the specific Turn 0/1/2 excerpt that motivates it. goal_1+2 trace to Turn 0 (the plan); goal_3+4 trace to Turn 1+2 (security elevation); goal_5+6 trace to Turn 0's Step 4-6 enumerations.

checks.check: no oracle-patch identifiers leak into goal text
checks.evidence: reference_patch.json is _status: no_canonical (stripped_export) — there IS no oracle patch to leak from. All module/primitive names referenced (secrets, anonymizer, REDACTED-marker, user_ prefix, etc.) appear verbatim in Turn 0's user-supplied plan, not in any oracle code. Every goal phrases identifiers as 'for instance' examples of mechanism.

checks.check: weights distinct from F2P weights in tests/test.sh and test_manifest.yaml
checks.evidence: Rubric weights (v3.4, unchanged from v3.2/v3.3 envelope; PATH (ii) sub-schedule inside goal_7 added): goal_1=0.30, goal_2=0.25, goal_3=0.15, goal_4=0.10, goal_5=0.05, goal_6=0.05, goal_7=0.10 (sum=1.00). F2P weights in tests/test.sh: t1_f2p_suite_runs_and_passes=0.12, t1_f2p_module_breadth=0.12, t1_f2p_config_behavior=0.12, t1_f2p_parser_or_cli_behavior=0.12, t2_f2p_secrets_redacted_constant=0.16, t2_f2p_anonymizer_hash_format=0.16, f2p_upstream_8138ab05=0.20. The rubric weights goal_1 highest (0.30, completeness sees broad suite as primary) while F2P weights the upstream py_compile gate highest (0.20, deterministic verifier sees file-existence as primary). Goal_7 (sandbox-feasible triage doc OR CLI invocation) has no F2P-gate counterpart — it is judge-only, by design. The two weight schemes are intentionally different and the rationale is documented in decomposition_notes. v3.4 graduates the goal_7 PATH (ii) sub-credit at 0.05/0.075/0.10 for 3/4/5+ findings — see goal_7.weight_schedule_v3_4.

Every goal (1-7) contains an explicit anti-gaming clause: goal_0 forbids placeholder/empty tests; goal_1 forbids assert-True padding and same-assertion duplication; goal_2 forbids import-only coverage; goal_3 forbids `assert REDACTED == REDACTED`; goal_4 forbids re-implementing the hash to compare against itself; goal_5 forbids `assert result is not None`-only tests; goal_6 forbids import-success-as-behavioral-coverage; goal_7 forbids single-sentence 'all findings addressed' and verbatim Step-8-list copying without per-finding dispositions, requires ≥5 distinct findings enumerated.

checks.check: goal_0 present as anti_effort floor at weight 0.0
checks.evidence: goal_0 tier='anti_effort', weight=0.0, with explicit semantics ('never adds credit, never subtracts from other goals' independent assessment, but signals non-credit if triggered').

checks.check: coverage of user asks (no orphan asks)
checks.evidence: Turn 0 'comprehensive suite' → goal_1; Turn 0 module enumeration + filename pinning → goal_2 (preferred filenames called out); Turn 0 Step 2 (secrets) → goal_3; Turn 0 Step 3 (anonymizer) → goal_4; Turn 0 Step 6 (config) → goal_5; Turn 0 Steps 4-5 (parser, cli) → goal_6; Turn 0 Step 8 (Triage Remaining Findings) → goal_7 (NEW in v3.2; v3.3 dual-path; v3.4 graduated PATH ii credit); Turn 1 (security) elevates goal_3+goal_4 weights; Turn 2 ('mark them all dealt with') → goal_7 (dual-path: literal CLI attempt OR sandbox-feasible doc proxy, latter graduated 3/4/5+). Out-of-scope: instruction.md Step 7 (source-mod Fix Findings) remains excluded by task design but note p2p_src_unmodified is a no-op against git-commit-discipline agents — see _provenance.KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified. Step 9 (Rescan) is covered under goal_7 PATH (i). KNOWN AUDIT FINDING (Gemini v2 round-1): scored 0.50 NO because Turn 2 reinterpretation was not surfaced enough in v3.0; v3.1 made the reinterpretation explicit. KNOWN AUDIT FINDING (Gemini v2 round-2): FAILed coverage_completeness because v3.1 left agents who DID do sandbox-feasible Step-8 triage work with no path to extra credit; v3.2 closes that gap with goal_7 at weight 0.10 (documentation proxy). KNOWN AUDIT FINDING (Gemini v2 round-3): FAILed coverage_completeness AND structured_output_pinned_when_user_did because v3.2's doc-only goal_7 left CLI-using trials uncredited; v3.3 made goal_7 dual-path. KNOWN REVIEW FINDING (Sonnet v3 final review, MINOR_PATCH 2026-06-06): the v3.3 DESLOPPIFY_INFEASIBILITY_VERIFICATION block misidentified the root cause (CLI is pip-installable mid-session, not categorically absent) and used adversarial framing; the PATH (ii) ≥5 threshold was slightly punitive at 0.10 weight; the p2p_src_unmodified gate was undocumented as a no-op against git-commit-discipline trials. v3.4 corrects all three: block renamed to DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION with accurate framing, PATH (ii) graduated to 3/4/5+ credit, p2p limitation surfaced in a new KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified block. Weight totals unchanged.
No oracle patch exists; nothing to be oracle-specific to. Goals were drafted from Turn 0's plan vocabulary and Turn 1+2's elevation, not from any reference diff.

_self_critique_log.trigger: Sonnet v3-rubric final review at analysis/v3_rubric_final_review_sonnet/dataclaw-test-coverage-fix.json (verdict: MINOR_PATCH, contested rubric). Three concrete patch directions: (1) the v3.3 DESLOPPIFY_INFEASIBILITY_VERIFICATION block is factually wrong about the root cause — desloppify is not pre-installed in the image at build time (the v3.3 ground_truth_about_dockerfile correct on that point) but IS pip-installable mid-session because the task has allow_internet=true and pip can reach PyPI; the Opus reference trial (siam9MG) verifies this empirically by running pip install desloppify during Turn 0 and then invoking desloppify resolve extensively. The block's adversarial framing about auditor 'hallucination' is uncharitable and operationally misleading. (2) The p2p_src_unmodified gate is a documented no-op against git-commit-discipline agents (the git-diff HEAD fallback returns clean when changes are committed). The gate is weight=0 P2P_REGRESSION in the manifest so it does NOT corrupt the reward, but the constraint it nominally names is not actually enforced. Worth documenting for judge/reviewer clarity. (3) The goal_7 PATH (ii) ≥5-finding binary threshold at 0.10 weight is slightly punitive — an agent that carefully documented 4 of 13 findings earns zero credit despite clear Step-8 engagement. Soften to graduated 3/4/5+ credit.
_self_critique_log.audit_fact_check: Sonnet's claims were verified against the Dockerfile and the Opus trial reward (0.7200, judge_score 0.87). The Dockerfile does not bake desloppify (`grep desloppify Dockerfile` → zero matches), but PyPI hosts it as a real 1.7 MB wheel, and the task environment has internet access (Harbor's claude-code installer requires it). Opus's trial demonstrates the pip-install-then-run path works. The p2p_src_unmodified gate's git-diff HEAD fallback is at test.sh:60 and Sonnet's reading is correct — committed changes don't appear in git diff HEAD, so SRC_OK=1 when an agent commits source edits; the gate's weight=0 in test_manifest.yaml means this does not affect F2P reward. All three Sonnet observations check out.
_self_critique_log.fix: Three changes, all confined to canonical_goals.json.v3.json (no test.sh / Dockerfile / instruction.md changes). (a) Renamed _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_INFEASIBILITY_VERIFICATION → DESLOPPIFY_AVAILABILITY_VERIFICATION and rewrote with the correct operational picture: not pre-installed but pip-installable, the Opus trial as empirical evidence, the implication that the CLI path does NOT trip p2p_src_unmodified because desloppify writes to its own finding DB rather than dataclaw/ source. Removed adversarial framing about auditor hallucination. Cross-references in goal_7 trace, _self_critique_passed.coverage_completeness evidence, in_scope_user_turns[2], and out_of_scope_user_turns_skipped updated to match. (b) Added _provenance.KNOWN_GATE_LIMITATION_p2p_src_unmodified block documenting the git-commit bypass, the zero scoring impact (P2P_REGRESSION weight=0), the judge's independent ability to detect source edits via final.patch, and three future remediation options (compare against base SHA, populate /baseline/dataclaw_src_hash, or explicitly accept the current behavior as advisory). (c) Added v3.4 graduated PATH (ii) sub-schedule to goal_7: 0/1/2 findings → 0.00, 3 findings → 0.05, 4 findings → 0.075, 5+ findings → 0.10. PATH (i) remains binary at full 0.10. The combine rule is MAX (not sum), preserving the 0.10 goal_7 envelope. decomposition_notes and _self_critique_passed.weights_distinct_from_F2P updated to reflect the new sub-schedule.
_self_critique_log.weight_changes: Total weight distribution UNCHANGED at the goal level: goal_1=0.30, goal_2=0.25, goal_3=0.15, goal_4=0.10, goal_5=0.05, goal_6=0.05, goal_7=0.10. Sum=1.00 verified. Inside goal_7's 0.10 envelope, PATH (ii) now has a 3-tier graduated schedule {3→0.05, 4→0.075, 5+→0.10} replacing the v3.3 binary {≥5→0.10}. PATH (i) unchanged (binary, 0.10).
_self_critique_log.expected_judge_behavior_post_fix: Judges who read the provenance block will no longer see adversarial framing about auditor hallucination — they will see an accurate availability picture (pip-installable, Opus trial as evidence) and can credit agents that ran desloppify regardless of how the agent acquired it. Trials that documented 4 findings in a triage.md (a hypothetical near-miss in v3.3) now earn 0.075 of 0.10 rather than 0. Trials that modified dataclaw/ source and committed will be assessed by the judge on goal_2 / goal_0 grounds via final.patch inspection — the rubric explicitly notes this is possible and the p2p gate's no-op status is documented rather than hidden. Expected aggregate effect: marginal upward adjustment for partial-documentation trials; no change for full-credit trials; no change for non-engaging trials. The Opus reference trial would still score 0.10 on goal_7 (PATH i full credit via pip install + desloppify resolve invocations) and would continue to be assessed on goals 1-6 independently.
no_changes_to: tests/test.sh (F2P gates unchanged; goal_7 remains judge-only; p2p_src_unmodified left as-is per scope — documented as a known no-op rather than fixed because the Dockerfile is READ_ONLY for this rubric patch and a true fix requires populating /baseline/dataclaw_src_hash at build time)
environment/Dockerfile (READ_ONLY per task authority; desloppify intentionally not baked in — agents who want it pip-install it)
canonical_goals.json (v2 manifest — referenced for traceability but not the live rubric; v3.4 supersedes via canonical_goals.json.v3.json)

_self_critique_log.trigger: Gemini v2 round-3 audit verdict at analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini_v2/dataclaw-test-coverage-fix.json (regression from 0.90 MOSTLY → 0.80 NO): FAIL on coverage_completeness (rationale: 'The rubric author incorrectly insists desloppify is missing, but the Opus trace proves it is installed and functional (/usr/local/bin/desloppify). By forcing a documentation proxy in goal_7, the rubric fails to score agents (like Opus) that successfully complete the actual triage using the CLI as requested.') AND FAIL on structured_output_pinned_when_user_did (rationale: 'goal_7 over-pins the acceptable solution to a documentation proxy (markdown file or docstrings) and provides no credit path for an agent that correctly uses the desloppify CLI to resolve the findings.'). single_biggest_risk: 'The judge will fail to reward agents that successfully complete the finding triage using the desloppify CLI because goal_7 incorrectly assumes the CLI is missing and only accepts a documentation proxy.'
_self_critique_log.audit_fact_check: Gemini's claim that 'desloppify IS installed and functional (/usr/local/bin/desloppify)' was re-verified against environment/Dockerfile and remains FACTUALLY INCORRECT. The Dockerfile installs python3, python3-pip, python3-venv, git, curl, ca-certificates, build-essential, tmux, asciinema (apt); pytest==8.3.4, pytest-cov==6.0.0, pytest-timeout==2.3.1, huggingface_hub==0.27.1 (pip); pip install -e .[dev] of dataclaw itself; and claude-code v2.1.108. `grep desloppify Dockerfile` → zero matches. The Opus trace likely contains either (a) a failed `desloppify` invocation attempt the auditor misread as success, (b) instruction.md Step 8/9 text quoted verbatim into the planning narration, or (c) hallucinated path strings in the agent's reasoning. The Dockerfile is ground truth. HOWEVER, fighting auditor hallucination message-by-message is a losing game; the structural fix is to make goal_7 robust to either factual reality.
_self_critique_log.fix: Rewrote goal_7 to accept TWO equivalent paths: PATH (i) any attempt to invoke `desloppify resolve` (intent-to-triage signal, success not required — visible in tool-call log even when the CLI is absent and returns `command not found`), PATH (ii) the original documentation/annotation proxy enumerating ≥5 of 13 Step-8 findings with per-finding dispositions. Either path alone earns full goal_7 credit (binary disposition; dual-path agents do not get double-credit). Added _provenance.DESLOPPIFY_INFEASIBILITY_VERIFICATION block documenting the Dockerfile ground truth + the auditor-hallucination root-cause analysis. Updated _provenance.in_scope_user_turns[2] Turn-2 prose, _provenance.out_of_scope_user_turns_skipped Step-8 entry, and _self_critique_passed.coverage_completeness evidence to reflect the dual-path acceptance.
_self_critique_log.what_goal_7_accepts_v3_3: PATH (i) DIRECT CLI ATTEMPT: any `desloppify resolve <disposition> ...` (or `desloppify scan`) invocation visible in tool-call log / bash history, regardless of exit code. PATH (ii) DOCUMENTATION PROXY: findings_status.md / triage.md / FINDINGS.md / notes.md with per-finding dispositions; or per-test docstrings/module comments naming finding ids each test covers; or tests/README.md mapping Step 8 to test files. Minimum 5 distinct findings for path (ii). Anti-gaming retained: path (i) requires actual execution (not just writing the string in markdown); path (ii) rejects single-sentence acks and verbatim-list-without-dispositions.
_self_critique_log.what_goal_7_does_NOT_require_v3_3: The agent does NOT need successful `desloppify` execution (CLI is intentionally absent per environment/Dockerfile). The agent does NOT need to do both paths — either alone suffices. The agent does NOT need to modify dataclaw/ source (still forbidden by p2p_src_unmodified). The agent does NOT need to cover all 13 findings — 5 is the floor for path (ii).
_self_critique_log.weight_changes: None. goal_7 weight remains 0.10. Total weight distribution unchanged: goal_1=0.30, goal_2=0.25, goal_3=0.15, goal_4=0.10, goal_5=0.05, goal_6=0.05, goal_7=0.10. Sum=1.00 verified.
_self_critique_log.expected_judge_behavior_post_fix: A judge who BELIEVES desloppify is installed will see Opus's trace contains `desloppify resolve` invocations and credit goal_7 via path (i) (no longer penalizes the trial). A judge who KNOWS desloppify is absent will see Opus's trace contains either failing CLI attempts (path i) or documentation work (path ii) and credit goal_7 accordingly. Either belief produces the correct outcome: trials that engaged with Step 8 in any visible way get the 0.10 credit, trials that did not get nothing. Expected aggregate effect on the audit score: coverage_completeness flips FAIL→PASS (CLI-using and doc-using agents both rewarded); structured_output_pinned_when_user_did flips FAIL→PASS (multiple acceptable solution forms now explicit); overall_score should return to 0.90+ MOSTLY/YES.
no_changes_to: tests/test.sh (F2P gates unchanged; goal_7 is judge-only)
environment/Dockerfile (desloppify deliberately absent — correct per task design; adding it would be out of scope for this rubric-only fix)

_self_critique_log.trigger: Gemini v2 round-2 audit verdict at analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini_v2/dataclaw-test-coverage-fix.json: FAIL on coverage_completeness with single_biggest_risk='The judge will fail to reward agents that successfully complete the finding triage (Step 8 / Turn 2) because the rubric incorrectly assumes the desloppify CLI is missing.'
_self_critique_log.audit_fact_check: Gemini's claim that 'desloppify IS installed and functional (/usr/local/bin/desloppify)' was checked against environment/Dockerfile and is FACTUALLY INCORRECT — the Dockerfile installs python3, pytest, huggingface_hub, claude-code v2.1.108, and no desloppify binary. However, the audit's UNDERLYING concern is valid: v3.1 routed all Turn-2 credit through the security-test goals (goal_3/goal_4), so an agent that explicitly engaged with Step 8's 13-item triage list (by writing a status doc or annotating tests with finding IDs) earned zero marginal credit for that documentation work.
_self_critique_log.fix: Added goal_7 (sandbox-feasible triage-proxy doc) at weight 0.10. Rebalanced goal_5 0.10 → 0.05 and goal_6 0.10 → 0.05 to fund it (those two were the lowest-priority Turn-0-only items per decomposition_notes, and goal_3/goal_4 already cover the security-elevated secondaries at 0.15 + 0.10). Weight sum verified: 0.30 + 0.25 + 0.15 + 0.10 + 0.05 + 0.05 + 0.10 = 1.00.
_self_critique_log.what_goal_7_accepts: Three sandbox-feasible artifact forms (any ONE suffices): (a) findings_status.md / triage.md / FINDINGS.md / notes.md with per-finding dispositions; (b) per-test docstrings or module-level comments naming the specific finding ids each test covers/accepts; (c) tests/README.md mapping Step 8's 13 findings to test files. Minimum 5 distinct findings enumerated. Anti-gaming: single-sentence 'all findings addressed' rejected; copying Step 8's verbatim list without per-finding dispositions rejected.
_self_critique_log.what_goal_7_does_NOT_require: The agent does NOT need to run `desloppify resolve` (CLI is intentionally absent per environment/Dockerfile). The agent does NOT need to modify dataclaw/ source files (still forbidden by p2p_src_unmodified). The agent does NOT need to cover all 13 findings — 5 is the floor.
_self_critique_log.expected_judge_behavior_post_fix: Trials like the Opus reference (verifier_reward 0.7200, judge_score 0.87, n_turns=7) that wrote a comprehensive test suite AND surfaced Step 8 triage in some form should now show a small (~0.05-0.10) judge_score uplift via goal_7. Trials that only wrote tests but never engaged with Step 8 should see no change (goal_7 zero, but goals 1-6 still score). The headline metric remains the test suite quality (goal_1+goal_2 = 0.55, more than half the weight).
no_changes_to: tests/test.sh (F2P gates unchanged; goal_7 is judge-only)
environment/Dockerfile (desloppify deliberately absent — correct per task design)

_dropped_from_v2.what_was_dropped: Nothing was structurally dropped from v2. v2 had 7 goals (goal_0 anti-effort + 6 graded goals); v3 has the same 7. All v2 goal IDs and tiers carry forward.
_dropped_from_v2.reason: v2's structural decomposition was sound: cores cover headline (suite + breadth), secondaries cover the four user-named primitives (redaction marker, hash format, config defaults, parser-or-cli value). The anti-effort floor was already present and correctly scoped.

_added_for_coverage.what_was_added: Explicit `trace` field per goal citing the specific Turn 0/1/2 user excerpt that motivates the goal
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 had rationale strings that referenced 'Turn 1 plan' / 'Turn 2 elevates security' but did not surface the verbatim user text. v3 makes the user-ask → goal trace machine-checkable.

_added_for_coverage.what_was_added: Per-goal anti-gaming clauses inside goal text (not just in goal_0)
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2's goal_0 floored non-engagement attempts but goals 1-6 themselves had no anti-gaming language — a clever trial could satisfy goal_5 via `assert load_config() is not None` and pass the judge. v3 inlines mechanism-based anti-gaming so each goal is gameable-only by actually covering the behavior.

_added_for_coverage.what_was_added: `_provenance` block documenting oracle-patch status (no_canonical), in-scope user turns, out-of-scope user turns, and an oracle-identifier audit
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 mentioned 'no oracle patch' inside its scanner_flags but did not surface the trace-basis as a first-class field. v3 makes it explicit that goal selection is user-ask-driven, not oracle-driven.

_added_for_coverage.what_was_added: `_self_critique_passed` block with 7 explicit checks (trace, no-oracle-IDs, weight-distinct-from-F2P, anti-gaming-in-goals, goal_0-present, coverage, nothing-oracle-specific)
_added_for_coverage.reason: Phase B requires self-critique. v3 surfaces the critique inline so a reviewer can verify each check rather than re-deriving them.

_added_for_coverage.what_was_added: Goal_3 weight raised from v2's 0.15 to v3's 0.15 (unchanged in value but rationale strengthened: Turn 1+2 elevation is now explicitly the justification for goal_3 > goal_4 weighting)
_added_for_coverage.reason: v2 had goal_3=0.15 and goal_4=0.10 but didn't explain the 0.05 gap. v3 ties the gap directly to the redaction marker being the more frequently-tested security primitive in Turn 0's plan (Step 2 lists 4+ marker-related test cases; Step 3 lists hash-format separately).
